\(\left(\dfrac{x+1}{x}\right)^2-\left(\dfrac{x-1}{x}\right)^2=\)
A. \(\dfrac{2}{x}\).
B. \(\dfrac{4}{x}\).
C. \(\dfrac{2}{x^2}\).
D. \(\dfrac{4}{x^2}\).
E. \(0\).

The radii of two solid spheres made of the same material are in the ratio \(2 : 3\). If the smaller sphere weight \(16\text{ kg}\), then the larger one weighs
A. \(24\text{ kg}\).
B. \(36\text{ kg}\).
C. \(48\text{ kg}\).
D. \(54\text{ kg}\).
E. \(60\text{ kg}\).

Given that \(x \neq 0\) and \(-x, x, 3x^2\) are in G.P., find \(x\).
A. \(-1\)
B. \(-\dfrac{1}{3}\)
C. \(\sqrt{3}\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\)
E. \(1\)

When the expression \(x^2+px+q\) is divided by \(x+1\), the remainder is \(4\). Find the value of \(2p-2q+1\).
A. \(-3\)
B. \(-5\)
C. \(-7\)
D. \(-9\)
E. It cannot be determined.

In the figure, the graph of \(y=x^2+bx+c\) cuts the x-axis at \(A\) and \(B\). \(OA+OB=\)
A. \(b\).
B. \(c\).
C. \(-b\).
D. \(-c\).
E. \(-\dfrac{b}{c}\).

\(ABCD\) is a trapezium in which \(AB \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} DC\), \(AB=8\text{ cm}\), \(DC=18\text{ cm}\), \(AD=BC=13\text{ cm}\). Find the area of the trapezium.
A. \(156\text{ cm}^2\)
B. \(169\text{ cm}^2\)
C. \(216\text{ cm}^2\)
D. \(312\text{ cm}^2\)
E. \(338\text{ cm}^2\)

A solid rectangular iron block, \(4\text{ cm} \times 2\text{ cm} \times 1\text{ cm}\), is melted and recast into a cube. The decrease in the total surface area is
A. \(1\text{ cm}^2\).
B. \(2\text{ cm}^2\).
C. \(3\text{ cm}^2\).
D. \(4\text{ cm}^2\).
E. \(5\text{ cm}^2\).

Figure a shows a circular measuring cylinder \(4\text{ cm}\) in diameter containing water. Three iron balls, each of diameter \(2\text{ cm}\), are dropped into the cylinder as shown in Figure b. What is the rise in the water level?
A. \(\dfrac{1}{4}\text{ cm}\)
B. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\text{ cm}\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\text{ cm}\)
D. \(1\text{ cm}\)
E. \(2\text{ cm}\)

Find, correct to the nearest dollar, the compound interest on \( \$ 10\,000 \) at \(8\%\) p.a. for \( 4 \) years, compounded half-yearly.
A. \( \$ 3\,200 \)
B. \( \$ 3\,605 \)
C. \( \$ 3\,686 \)
D. \( \$ 13\,200 \)
E. \( \$ 13\,686 \)

If the selling price of \( 5 \) pens is the same as the cost price of \( 6 \) pens, the percentage profit in selling a pen will be
A. \(16\dfrac{2}{3}\%\).
B. \(20\%\).
C. \(60\%\).
D. \(116\dfrac{2}{3}\%\).
E. \(120\%\).

The circumference of a circle is \(6\pi\text{ cm}\). The length of an arc of the circle which subtends an angle of \(\dfrac{1}{3}\) radian at the centre is
A. \(1\text{ cm}\).
B. \(\dfrac{3}{2}\text{ cm}\).
C. \(2\text{ cm}\).
D. \(\pi\text{ cm}\).
E. \(2\pi\text{ cm}\).

In the figure, \( \angle A = 75^\circ \), \( \angle B = 45^\circ \) and \( CD \) bisects \( \angle ACB \). \( \dfrac{BD}{CD} = \)
A. \( \dfrac{2}{3}\).
B. \( \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\).
C. \( \sqrt{2} \).
D. \( \sqrt{\dfrac{2}{3}}\).
E. \( \sqrt{\dfrac{3}{2}}\).

A rectangle is \(6\text{ cm}\) long and \(8\text{ cm}\) wide. The acute angle between its diagonals, correct to the nearest degree is
A. \(37^\circ\).
B. \(41^\circ\).
C. \(49^\circ\).
D. \(74^\circ\).
E. \(83^\circ\).

In the figure, chords \(AC\) and \(BD\) meet at \(E\) and \(AB \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} DC\). If \(\angle CED=104^\circ\), find \(\angle ABD\).
A. \(76^\circ\)
B. \(52^\circ\)
C. \(38^\circ\)
D. \(14^\circ\)
E. It cannot be determined.

In the figure, \(BD=a\), \(DC=b\) and the area of \(\Delta ABC\) is \(s\).
A. \(\dfrac{s(a+b)}{a}\)
B. \(\dfrac{s(a+b)}{b}\)
C. \(\dfrac{s(a+b)^2}{a^2}\)
D. \(\dfrac{s(a+b)^2}{b^2}\)
E. \(\dfrac{s(a^2+b^2)}{a^2}\)

The real number \(\pi\) is
A. \(\dfrac{22}{7}\).
B. \(3.1416\).
C. the ratio of the area of a circle to the square of its diameter.
D. the ratio of the circumference of a circle to its radius.
E. the ratio of the circumference of a circle to its diameter.

In the figure, \(AB\), \(BC\) and \(CD\) are three equal chords of a circle. If \(\angle BAC=a\), then \(\angle AED=\)
A. \(2a\).
B. \(3a\).
C. \(90^\circ-a\).
D. \(180^\circ-2a\).
E. \(180^\circ-3a\).

In the figure, \(ABCD\) and \(ABEF\) are parallelograms. \(\dfrac{\text{Area of } ABCD}{\text{Area of } ABEF}=\)
A. \(\dfrac{AD}{AF}\).
B. \(\dfrac{BC}{BF}\).
C. \(\dfrac{BC}{EF}\).
D. \(\dfrac{AD^2}{AF^2}\).
E. \(\dfrac{BC^2}{EF^2}\).

In the figure, I, II and III are equilateral triangles. Area of I : Area of II : Area of III =
A. \(\alpha : \beta : \gamma\).
B. \(\sin\alpha : \sin\beta : \sin\gamma\).
C. \(\sin^2\alpha : \sin^2\beta : \sin^2\gamma\).
D. \(\cos\alpha : \cos\beta : \cos\gamma\).
E. \(\cos^2\alpha : \cos^2\beta : \cos^2\gamma\).

Which of the following straight lines divide(s) the circle \((x-1)^2+(y+1)^2=1\) into two equal parts?
I. \(x-y-2=0\)
II. \(x+y+2=0\)
III. \(x-y+2=0\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

The equation of a circle is \(x^2+y^2-4x+2y+1=0\). Which of the following is/are true?
I. The centre is \((-2, 1)\).
II. The radius is \(2\) units.
III. The circle intersects the y-axis at two distinct points.
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

Two perpendicular lines \(kx+y-4=0\) and \(x-2y+3=0\) intersect at the point \((h, k)\). Find \(h\) and \(k\).
A. \(h=-7, k=-2\)
B. \(h=-2, k=\dfrac{1}{2}\)
C. \(h=1, k=2\)
D. \(h=-4, k=-\dfrac{1}{2}\)
E. \(h=-3, k=2\)

If the median of the 5 different integers \(2, 7, 10, x, 2x-3\) is \(7\), then \(x=\)
A. \(3\).
B. \(4\).
C. \(5\).
D. \(6\).
E. \(8\).

I.
II.
III.
The figures show the histograms of the three frequency distributions. Arrange their standard deviations in ascending order of magnitude.
A. I, II, III
B. I, III, II
C. II, I, III
D. II, III, I
E. III, II, I

One letter is taken from each of the words "MAN" and "ART" at random. Find the probability that the two letters are not the same.
A. \(\dfrac{1}{9}\)
B. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\)
C. \(\dfrac{4}{9}\)
D. \(\dfrac{2}{3}\)
E. \(\dfrac{8}{9}\)

Four persons \(A, B, C, D\) sit randomly around a round table. The probability that \(A\) sits next to \(B\) is
A. \(\dfrac{1}{4}\).
B. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\).
C. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\).
D. \(\dfrac{2}{3}\).
E. \(\dfrac{5}{6}\).

A die is thrown twice. Find the probability that the number obtained at the first throw is greater than that at the second throw.
A. \(\dfrac{1}{6}\)
B. \(\dfrac{5}{12}\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\)
D. \(\dfrac{7}{12}\)
E. \(\dfrac{5}{6}\)

Peter bought an article for \( \$ x\). He sold it to Mary at a profit of \(20\%\). Mary then sold it to John for \( \$ 90\) at a loss of \(25\%\). Find \(x\).
A. \(56.25\)
B. \(81\)
C. \(90\)
D. \(100\)
E. \(144\)

If \(x\) and \(y\) are integers with \(x \gt y\), which of the following is/are true?
I. \(x^2 \gt y^2\)
II. \(\dfrac{1}{x} \lt \dfrac{1}{y}\)
III. \(10^x \gt 10^y\)
A. III only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

Solve the inequality \(x\log_{10} 0.1 \gt \log_{10} 10\).
A. \(x \gt -1\)
B. \(x \gt 1\)
C. \(x \gt 100\)
D. \(x \lt 1\)
E. \(x \lt -1\)

The figure shows the graph of \(y=ax^2+bx+c\). Which of the following is/are true?
I. \(a \gt 0\)
II. \(b \gt 0\)
III. \(c \gt 0\)
A. I only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. I and II only
E. I, II and III

A man walks from place \(A\) to place \(B\) at a speed of \(3\text{ km/h}\) and cycles immediately back to place \(A\) along the same road at a speed of \(15\text{ km/h}\). The average speed for the whole trip is
A. \(5\text{ km/h}\).
B. \(6\text{ km/h}\).
C. \(9\text{ km/h}\).
D. \(10\text{ km/h}\).
E. \(12\text{ km/h}\).

Let \(n\) be a positive integer. Which of the following number is/are odd?
I. \(2^{2n+1}\)
II. \(2^n+1\)
III. \(3(2^n)\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

In the figure, \(O\) is the centre of the circle. \(a+b=\)
A. \(180^\circ\).
B. \(c\).
C. \(\dfrac{c}{2}\).
D. \(180^\circ-c\).
E. \(180^\circ-\dfrac{c}{2}\).

In the figure, \(AD=p, CD=q\) and \(\angle B=90^\circ\), \(BC=\)
A. \(p\sin\theta-q\sin\phi\).
B. \(p\sin\theta-q\cos\phi\).
C. \(p\cos\theta-q\sin\phi\).
D. \(p\sin\theta+q\cos\phi\).
E. \(p\cos\theta+q\sin\phi\).

In the figure, \(O\) is the centre of the circle. If \(AB=12\) and \(AC=13\), then \(\cos\theta=\)
A. \(\dfrac{5}{12}\).
B. \(\dfrac{5}{13}\).
C. \(\dfrac{12}{13}\).
D. \(\dfrac{12}{25}\).
E. \(\dfrac{13}{25}\).

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a rectangle inclined at an angle of \(30^\circ\) to the horizontal plane \(ABEF\). \(\angle CBD=60^\circ\). Let \(\theta\) be the inclination of \(BD\) to the horizontal plane. \(\sin\theta=\)
A. \(\dfrac{1}{4}\).
B. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\).
C. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\).
D. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{3}\).
E. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\).

How many different values of \(x\) between \(0^\circ\) and \(360^\circ\) will satisfy the equation \((\sin x+1)(2\sin x+1)=0\)?
A. \(0\)
B. \(1\)
C. \(2\)
D. \(3\)
E. \(4\)

If \(0^\circ \leq x \lt 360^\circ\), the number of points of intersection of the graph of \(y=\sin x\) and \(y=1+\cos x\) is
A. \(0\).
B. \(1\).
C. \(2\).
D. \(3\).
E. \(4\).

In the figure, \( O \) is the centre of the circle of radius \( 4 \). The area of the shaded region is
A. \(\dfrac{4\pi}{3}-4\).
B. \(\dfrac{4\pi}{3}-8\).
C. \(\dfrac{4\pi}{3}-4\sqrt{3}\).
D. \(\dfrac{2\pi}{3}-4\).
E. \(\dfrac{8\pi}{3}-8\).

In the figure, \(C\) is the centre of the circle. \(ABCD\) is a straight line. \(AQR\) touches the circle at \(Q\). If \(\angle DAR=20^\circ\), then \(\angle DQR=\)
A. \(35^\circ\).
B. \(40^\circ\).
C. \(55^\circ\).
D. \(65^\circ\).
E. \(70^\circ\).

In the figure, \(DE \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} BC\) and \(AB \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} EF\). If \(AE:EC=1:2\), then area of \(\Delta ADE :\) area of parallelogram \(BFED=\)
A. \(1:2\).
B. \(1:3\).
